description: Idler gear assembly: spur gear on a stepped cylindrical post rising from a square base plate, centered at XY origin, with central bore and mounting holes
components: 16-tooth spur gear with star-polygon tooth profile
Four 6mm mounting holes through base plate

features.description: 5 hole(s) of diameter 8mm central bore; 6mm x4 mounting holes mm

notes: 1. Base plate: box from (-30,-30,0) to (30,30,5). 2. Lower post: cylinder radius=10, from Z=5 to Z=18, centered at (0,0). 3. Retaining shoulder: cylinder radius=7, from Z=18 to Z=40, centered at (0,0). 4. Gear: modeled as an extruded star-polygon profile from Z=10 to Z=18; outer radius=20mm (tip), inner radius=16mm (root), 16 teeth — implement as a union of the root cylinder (r=16) extruded 8mm with 16 rectangular/trapezoidal tooth profiles evenly spaced around the circumference, OR use a polygon approximation alternating between tip radius 20mm and root radius 16mm at 16 tooth intervals (32 vertices total, alternating r=20 and r=16 at angular steps of 360/32=11.25 degrees). 5. Central bore: subtract cylinder radius=4 from Z=0 to Z=40 through entire assembly. 6. Four mounting holes: subtract cylinders radius=3 from Z=0 to Z=5 at (±22, ±22). 7. The gear bore matches the lower post from Z=10 to Z=18 — the gear sits around the lower post, so the gear's inner hole should be radius=10 to fit on the post (the 8mm central bore passes through the post itself). 8. Gear inner bore should be 20mm diameter (radius=10) to fit over the lower post, not 8mm — the 8mm bore is the axle bore through the post center. 9. Volume estimate is approximate; exact value depends on gear tooth geometry implementation.
